stories.headline: US and Iran launch fresh strikes as peace efforts continue
stories.summary: Washington and Tehran are still trying to formalise a ceasefire

stories.headline: US launches weekend strikes on Iran as Trump reportedly tightens framework of draft deal
stories.summary: Any tweaks to the draft could further delay an agreement to formally end the Middle East war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz after weeks of fraught negotiations marked by sharp rhetoric and occasional flare-ups of violence.

stories.headline: Israel airstrike kills at least two Palestinians at Gaza port
stories.summary: At least two Palestinians were killed and around a dozen wounded when an Israeli air strike hit a crowded cafe.

stories.headline: Iran war live: US bombs Qeshm, Goruk; Kuwait reports missile, drone attacks
stories.summary: France requests UN Security Council meeting over Israel's invasion of Lebanon, as Iran says talks with US continue.

stories.headline: Trump ally and leftist candidate head for runoff in Colombia's presidential election
stories.summary: The right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella, an admirer of Donald Trump, came first in the vote, followed closely by left-wing senator Iván Cepeda, backed by current president Gustavo Petro.

stories.headline: France and allies intercept suspected Russian shadow fleet tanker, Macron says
stories.summary: Russia has reportedly built up a flotilla of old oil tankers of opaque ownership to get around sanctions imposed by the European Union over Moscow's 2022 full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: Iran and US report new wave of strikes in Gulf
stories.summary: Iran and the US say they have carried out fresh attacks around the Strait of Hormuz, the third known escalation in a week.

stories.headline: Colombia presidential runoff pits leftist senator against pro-Trump rival
stories.summary: Left-wing senator Iván Cepeda will face Trump admirer Abelardo de la Espriella at the final ballot on 21 June.

stories.headline: Israel Expands Operation in Lebanon
stories.summary: Israel expanded its ground assault in Lebanon with its broadest incursion into the country in a quarter-century as Hezbollah stepped up attacks on Israel’s north. On Sunday, Defense Minister Israel Katz said the IDF had planted a flag on the historic Beaufort castle near Nabatieh, and that the expansion amounted to “a permanent presence” in the region. Bloomberg's Dana Khraiche reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Colombia’s far-right presidential candidate Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
stories.summary: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeksThe far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella won the first round of Colombia’s presidential election on Sunday and will face senator Iván Cepeda, the candidate backed by leftwing president Gustavo Petro, in the runoff.With 99.97% of ballots counted, the outsider and Donald Trump admirer Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just ove

stories.headline: Tense election night in Colombia as country heads to presidential runoff
stories.summary: Colombia’s election heads to a June 21 runoff after a tight first round between de la Espriella and Cepeda.

stories.headline: Oil prices climb as Israel expands military operation in Lebanon
stories.summary: Oil prices rose on Monday morning after Israel stepped up its military operation in Lebanon.

stories.headline: Europe is running from a phantom China threat
stories.summary: EU leaders fear cheap Chinese exports when they should be worrying about embedded spyware and rare earths dependency

stories.headline: Jay Powell warns Federal Reserve is undergoing ‘stress test’
stories.summary: Former chair says Donald Trump’s attempts to fire central bankers undermine the rule of law

stories.headline: SoftBank overtakes Toyota after more than 20 years to become Japan’s largest company
stories.summary: Demand for AI stocks powers tech giant’s shares as it takes top spot by market capitalisation

stories.headline: UK House Prices Drop as Mortgage Costs Bite, Nationwide Says
stories.summary: UK house prices fell at the fastest pace for almost a year, according to one of Britain’s biggest mortgage lenders, suggesting the surge in borrowing costs triggered by the Iran war is placing a strain on prospective homebuyers.

stories.headline: Fed's Powell On the Need for Independence
stories.summary: Bloomberg MLIV strategist Mark Cranfield discusses Powell's speech as he accepted the John F. Kennedy Profile in Courage Award. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: France Boards Another Tanker Tied to Russian Oil Trade
stories.summary: France’s navy boarded another oil tanker on the high seas after it sailed from Russia, the latest move in a global crackdown on shadow-fleet vessels used to export sanctioned crude.

stories.headline: EM Stocks Hit Record High on AI, Currencies Lower as Oil Climbs
stories.summary: Emerging Asian equities rose to a record, propelled by continuing enthusiasm for the artificial intelligence trade that’s bolstered global stocks despite the Iran war.

stories.headline: Ex-US Fed Chair Powell warns against politicisation amid Trump’s attacks
stories.summary: Jerome Powell says the US central bank is undergoing a 'stress test' like other institutions in the current era.

stories.headline: SoftBank dethrones Toyota as Japan's most valuable company
Huge blast kills dozens in rebel-held village in Myanmar
stories.summary: Insurgents say it was caused by explosives being used for mining close to the Chinese border.

stories.headline: Poland's controversial 'Highway to Hel' 666 bus revived
stories.summary: Poland's seaside resort called Hel is getting its 666 bus service back, despite earlier opposition from Christian groups.

stories.headline: Xi’s last frontier: China’s plan to transform its west
stories.summary: A vast development drive aims to tap the region’s economic potential and extend Beijing’s control

stories.headline: Danish shipyard still servicing LNG tankers for Russia trade
stories.summary: Fayard is only remaining EU yard taking on dry-dock work for ships carrying gas exported from Siberia

stories.headline: EasyJet Calls Castlelake Approach ‘Highly Opportunistic’
stories.summary: EasyJet Plc’s board called the overture by investment firm Castlelake LP “highly opportunistic” as the UK budget carrier grapples with a depressed stock price, cautioning that attempting a takeover would be fraught with risk.

stories.headline: Hiab Agrees to Acquire Truck Maker Labrie for $1.04 Billion
stories.summary: Finnish-listed Hiab Oyj has agreed to acquire refuse collection vehicle manufacturer Labrie Environmental Group at an enterprise value of $1.04 billion, strengthening its position in the North American waste and recycling market.

stories.headline: India Stock Trading Value Hits Record as MSCI Changes Spur Deals
stories.summary: Trading value in India’s cash equities surged to an all-time high Friday, as MSCI Inc.’s index changes triggered a flurry of bulk deals by global passive funds.

stories.headline: Risk Rally Powers Past Iran Stalemate | The Asia Trade 6/1/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Sydney with Haidi Stroud-Watts and Paul Allen, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Copper Gains With US Tariffs Deadline Less Than a Month Away
stories.summary: Copper advanced in New York and London to kick off a crucial month that should deliver more clarity on the Trump administration’s plans for levies on imports of the metal to the US.

stories.headline: Aussie Risks More Losses Versus Kiwi on Rate View, Analysts Say
stories.summary: The Australian dollar may have further scope to fall against the kiwi as traders unwind bearish positions in New Zealand’s currency following a hawkish shift from the nation’s central bank, analysts said.

stories.headline: SMBC Nikko Mulls Launching $627 Million Japan Mezzanine Fund
stories.summary: SMBC Nikko Securities Inc. is considering establishing a fund with One Investment Management to provide so-called mezzanine financing, people familiar with the matter said.

stories.headline: Korea Boosts Bond-Market Vigil Via Daily Phone Calls, Group Chat
stories.summary: South Korea intensified monitoring of the government bond market through daily phone calls and a private messaging group with market participants, as authorities step up efforts to contain rising yields.

stories.headline: As Democrats Worry About Senate Race, Platner Attacks Reports About Sexual Messages
stories.summary: Graham Platner, whose contest in Maine is a key to Democrats’ hopes of winning the Senate, sought to discredit reports that he had exchanged sexual messages with women outside his marriage.

stories.summary: Follow today’s news liveGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastTony Abbott, the new party president of the Liberals, said he wouldn’t get “too excited” by the poll showing One Nation’s surging support. How to prevent the 'K-economy' from becoming more K-shaped

stories.summary: Arsenal fans packed London's streets as the men's Premier League winners and women's champions celebrated a successful season together.

stories.headline: Road freight: The silent drivers of trade face high fuel prices and an ageing workforce
stories.summary: Despite rising fuel costs and mass shortages of drivers, Lyall Cresswell, CEO of the Transport Exchange Group is still confident that “there's a lot more efficiency [to] eke out of the market”.

stories.headline: Israel seizes castle in Lebanon as it expands ground offensive
stories.summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu calls the capture of the strategic fortress a "decisive shift" in Israel's campaign against Hezbollah, as European governments criticise the escalation.

stories.headline: Ebola spread in DR Congo 'deeply alarming', MSF warns
stories.summary: The medical charity's comments come as the head of the World Health Organization visits the region worst-hit by the virus outbreak.

stories.headline: Russia launches fresh strikes on Ukraine, Kyiv hits refinery hub
stories.summary: In response to Russian shelling of Ukrainian territory, Ukrainian forces launched a series of powerful strikes on strategically important Russian oil infrastructure.

stories.summary: The left-wing senator and far-right newcomer will face each other in a run-off on June 21, with security a top issue.

stories.headline: PSG: Champions League winners celebrate return to Paris after Budapest triumph
stories.summary: PSG players were warmly welcomed and celebrated by supporters and authorities in Paris upon their return from Budapest after their Champions League triumph

stories.headline: Caribbean hot sauce producers warn of shortages and higher prices
stories.summary: Manufacturers in Jamaica say the key chilli peppers they need are in limited supply.

stories.headline: Stocks Hit Record on AI Rally, Oil Rises on Iran: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks rose to a record as investors doubled down on the artificial intelligence trade that’s powered equities to all-time highs. Oil climbed as a US-Iran ceasefire deal remained elusive.

stories.headline: Trump Is Dominating GOP Primary Elections. Does That Mean Victory in the Midterms?
stories.summary: Come November, the Republican Party will need the support of voters outside of President Trump’s base, many of whom are deeply dissatisfied with the economy and the Iran war.

stories.headline: WHO calls for community cooperation to contain Ebola outbreak in DRC
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus makes appeal after protests against protocols for handling victims’ bodies in Ituri provinceContaining the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo requires community cooperation and is “everybody’s business”, the World Health Organization has said.Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the organisation’s director general, made the plea on Sunday during a visit to eastern Congo where some residents have protested against

stories.headline: Japan defence minister rebuffs claims of ‘new militarism’ levelled by China
stories.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi says Japan valued as a ‘peace-loving’ nation while China expands military capabilities ‘without sufficient transparency’Japan’s defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing’s criticism of Tokyo’s increasingly muscular security stance.Under the prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, Japan has quickened its pivot to a more proactive defence policy, further shaking off

stories.headline: French police arrest 780 after violent clashes as PSG fans celebrate Champions League win
stories.summary: Interior minister says 57 officers injured as rioters set fires and vandalise shops in about 15 citiesFrench police have detained 780 people involved in violent clashes in Paris and other French cities that erupted on Saturday night after Paris Saint-Germain defeated Arsenal to win the Champions League.The interior minister, ­Laurent Nuñez, said 57 officers were wounded, with most suffering minor injuries, as football fans set off fires and vanda

stories.headline: Ethiopia to vote in first elections since Tigray peace deal
stories.summary: Four years after the formal end of Ethiopia's Tigray war, Ethiopians will be voting in general elections.

stories.headline: Ethiopia’s election is about affirming national commitment to democracy
stories.summary: A national vote alone can't address all challenges in the country, but it can be a step toward democratic consolidation.

stories.headline: Polls open in Colombia’s pivotal presidential election
stories.summary: Colombians headed to the polls to choose a new president in a high-stakes election.

stories.headline: Hungary: Tamás Sulyok will not resign as president
stories.summary: The government’s deadline for his resignation passed on Sunday night, but Sulyok said he would wait for the Venice Commission’s opinion and insisted that the matter should be handled in line with the constitution.

stories.headline: South Africa made to look like fools over World Cup visa issues, says minister
stories.summary: After a day of uncertainty, the government confirms the team will depart for Mexico on Monday.

stories.headline: TSMC’s Local Investors Narrow Valuation Gap With Wall Street
stories.summary: Local investors are driving the premium on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.’s US-listed shares to a two-year low as they pile into the chipmaker’s stock in Taipei on bets the AI boom has further to run.

stories.headline: Bond Trader Bets on Fed Hike Poised for Gut Check From Jobs Data
stories.summary: Bond traders are looking to a key jobs report this week to confirm their wagers that the US economy is strong enough to push the Federal Reserve to lift interest rates by next year.

stories.headline: SpaceX’s IPO Forces Wall Street to Reorganize Around It
stories.summary: The company’s ambitious listing plan is set to clear the way for other mega-offerings. It also risks threatening the integrity of the market itself

stories.headline: Release of Tina Peters, Election Denier and Former County Clerk, Divides Colorado City
stories.summary: The former clerk of Mesa County is scheduled to be set free on Monday, after her nine-year prison sentence was commuted by Gov. Jared Polis.

stories.headline: Trump Administration Sees Striking Exodus of Legal Talent
stories.summary: The departure of more than 10,000 federal lawyers has left some agencies without sufficient staff and has boosted the ranks of state attorneys general offices and advocacy groups.

stories.headline: After Voting Decision, a Month of Political Earthquakes Across the South
stories.summary: As Republicans rush to redraw the region’s congressional maps, some voters are confused and concerned, and civil rights activists are gearing up for the fight of a generation.

stories.summary: New Jersey orders curfew after immigration protests escalate.

stories.headline: What’s at stake in Ethiopia’s elections?
stories.summary: Ethiopia's governing party is seeking to cement its grip on power amid a fragmented electorate.

stories.headline: How Putin became master of the image
stories.summary: From enigmatic KGB agent to wartime ruler, this is how Putin has repeatedly reinvented his image, and himself.

stories.headline: Bound by blood: new film highlights Jamaica’s outlawed obeah belief system
stories.summary: Stew Peas focuses on obeah, an enduring African magic practice in Jamaica banned by colonisers in the 1700sA new movie from award-winning Jamaican film-maker Sosiessia Nixon shines a spotlight on Jamaica’s enduring west African-based magic and spiritual healing tradition known as obeah.Nixon’s tense, feature-length suspense, Stew Peas, tells of the story of Jamaican detective Tessa, who is obsessed with an old murder case. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Ecuador accused of meddling in Colombian election with tariff vow
stories.summary: Ecuadorian leader Daniel Noboa offers to cancel tariffs after speaking to a right-wing presidential candidate ahead of Sunday's vote.

stories.headline: Moana Pasifika sign off from Super Rugby with a win and emotionally charged hymn
stories.summary: Undermanned side upsets finals-bound ACT Brumbies 21-19Players come together to mark victory and club’s expected demiseAn undermanned Moana Pasifika have capped off their potential final match with a stirring victory, upsetting the finals-bound ACT Brumbies 21-19.But there were mixed feelings as players celebrated a rare win before coming together with staff to mark the occasion of the club’s farewell game and expected demise with an emotionally

stories.headline: Netanyahu says he has directed IDF to increase control of Gaza to 70%
stories.summary: The expansion in control by Israel would contradict the terms of the ceasefire  Israel and Hamas agreed to in October 2025.

stories.headline: US and Iran 'very close' to deal but 'not there yet', Vance says
stories.summary: US officials earlier told the BBC that the framework of a ceasefire extension deal had been agreed, pending the approval of Trump and Iran's leadership.

stories.headline: Israel put on UN sexual violence in warzones blacklist for first time
stories.summary: Israel says it rejects allegations in a new report by the office of the UN secretary general.

stories.headline: Mumbai's famed dabbawalas fed millions for over 100 years - now they are disappearing
stories.summary: Dabbawalas, who deliver home-cooked meals, are leaving the trade as remote work and rising costs threaten their future.

stories.headline: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
stories.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

stories.headline: How Cuba is addressing its housing crisis with shipping containers
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant visited Barrio Toledo, where at least 700 containers are being repurposed into two-bedroom homes with kitchens, bathrooms and patios.

stories.headline: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
stories.summary: Hamas says Mohammed Odeh was killed alongside his wife and two children in a strike on a residential building.

stories.headline: The world's carmakers are struggling to compete with China
stories.summary: The BBC visited China’s EV factories and found they are dominating the ecosystems shaping the global auto industry.

stories.headline: Bolivian Congress allows deployment of troops to quell protests

stories.headline: Iran condemns US strikes as 'gross violation' of ceasefire
stories.summary: The attacks took place while Iranian and Qatari negotiators were in Doha for peace talks.

stories.headline: US launches new strikes on Iran, targeting missile sites and boats
stories.summary: US Central Command says the strikes were taken in "self-defence", while Iran reports it downed a US drone that entered its airspace.

stories.headline: Can EU find a Russia whisperer to mediate an end to war in Ukraine?
stories.summary: With the US pulling out of trilateral talks with Russia and Ukraine, the EU is looking for potential candidates to step in.

stories.headline: Trump tells US negotiators 'not to rush' into deal with Iran
stories.summary: The deal under discussion would involve a 60-day ceasefire extension during which the Strait of Hormuz would be reopened, according to US media.
